error_database.issue: Duplicate b:includable id='postCommentsLink' in Blog1 widget
error_database.symptom: Blogger rejects theme import with validation error: 'includable with id postCommentsLink declared more than once'
error_database.root_cause: Includable lists were composed from separate widget documentation sections (post section vs comments section) that both listed postCommentsLink
error_database.bad_code_pattern: Two <b:includable id='postCommentsLink'> elements in the same <b:widget id='Blog1'>
error_database.fix: Removed one duplicate includable, kept <b:includable id='postCommentsLink'><b:comment>Disabled</b:comment></b:includable> once
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-001: All b:includable IDs within a single widget must be unique

error_database.issue: Incorrect widget setting names for BlogArchive v2: displayStyle, showType, showPostsCount
error_database.symptom: Widget settings silently ignored by Blogger; archive widget may not function as expected
error_database.root_cause: Version 2 widgets use different setting names than version 1: displayStyle→showStyle, showType→frequency, showPostsCount→showPosts
error_database.fix: Renamed to showStyle, frequency, showPosts per Blogger v2 widget API
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-002: Always use version='2' setting names for modern widget templates

error_database.issue: Incorrect version string format: version='2' not '2'
error_database.symptom: Widget may not use v2 template; setting names may be ignored
error_database.root_cause: Version attribute on BlogArchive widget used '2' (string) instead of '2' (number)
error_database.bad_code_pattern: <b:widget ... type='BlogArchive' version='2'> with v1 setting names
error_database.fix: Kept version='2' (correct value) and updated setting names to match
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-002: Version value must match the widget API version

error_database.issue: Hero section not centered on mobile; text overflow on small screens
error_database.symptom: Hero text clipped on ≤480px screens; CTA buttons not stacking vertically
error_database.root_cause: No mobile-specific grid/flex overrides in responsive CSS
error_database.fix: Added max-width constraints, centered text, stacked CTAs at ≤480px
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-006: Every section must be tested at all 5 breakpoints

error_database.issue: Inconsistent section spacing across breakpoints
error_database.symptom: Some sections had --space-5xl padding, others --space-4xl; no responsive reduction
error_database.root_cause: No spacing scale applied to section padding; raw values used
error_database.fix: Applied --space-6xl (desktop), --space-5xl (1199px), --space-4xl (991px), --space-3xl (767px), --space-3xl (480px)
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-007: Use design token spacing scale; no raw values

error_database.issue: Heading sizes did not decrease on mobile; line-height too tight
error_database.symptom: H1 looked proportionally too large on phone screens
error_database.root_cause: clamp() values not tuned; heading line-heights set to 1.0
error_database.fix: Tuned clamp ranges: H1 clamp(2.75rem, 5vw, 4.75rem), H2 clamp(1.875rem, 3.5vw, 3rem), line-height 1.1 for H1
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-008: Use clamp() for fluid typography; verify at every breakpoint

error_database.issue: Hero content lacked wrapper for responsive control
error_database.symptom: Gradient overlays and text overlapped on narrow screens
error_database.root_cause: Hero used absolute positioning for decorative elements without containment
error_database.fix: Added .hero-content wrapper with relative positioning; stacked elements vertically on mobile
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-009: Hero content must use wrapper div for responsive control

error_database.issue: Service cards had different heights; content overflow on short cards
error_database.symptom: Cards in same row misaligned; layout looked uneven
error_database.root_cause: Cards used default stretch alignment but content heights varied
error_database.fix: Added display:flex; flex-direction:column; on cards; flex:1 on content area
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-010: Card grid items must use flex-column layout for equal height

error_database.issue: Sidebar widget titles lacked visual anchor
error_database.symptom: Widget titles floated without separator; hard to distinguish sections
error_database.root_cause: No border-bottom or background on widget title elements
error_database.fix: Added border-bottom: 2px solid var(--primary-light) on .sidebar h3
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-011: Sidebar widget titles must have visual separator

error_database.issue: Testimonials and dark sections lacked visual depth
error_database.symptom: Cards on dark backgrounds looked flat without shadows
error_database.root_cause: No inner shadow or border on cards in dark sections
error_database.fix: Added border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06) + subtle shadow on dark cards
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-012: Dark section cards need border/shadow for depth

error_database.issue: Contact form styling clashed with hero; CTA buttons too small
error_database.symptom: Contact form looked generic; no lead-gen focus
error_database.root_cause: Contact form used same styling as generic form components
error_database.fix: Applied squared buttons (--radius-sm), gradient border wrapper, lead-gen optimized layout
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-013: Contact form must use lead-gen optimized styling

error_database.issue: Footer columns lacked hierarchy; social icons too prominent
error_database.symptom: Footer had no visual organization; social section dominated
error_database.root_cause: No column width assignment; social links placed before content columns
error_database.fix: Added grid-template-columns: 2fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; moved social to brand column
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-014: Footer must have grid hierarchy: brand(2fr) + columns(1fr each)

error_database.issue: No centralized design token system; raw values everywhere
error_database.symptom: Inconsistent spacing, colors, shadows across components
error_database.root_cause: Theme was built without CSS custom property abstraction
error_database.fix: Created 40+ design tokens: --space-*, --text-*, --shadow-*, --radius-*, --leading-*, --tracking-*, --primary-bg, --accent-light
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-015: All CSS values must reference custom property tokens

error_database.issue: Config variables and incorrect Blogger bindings leaking into navbar; header whitespace; misaligned layout; full blog title in brand
error_database.symptom: Header has 3 bugs: (1) config variables like disqusShortname render in nav, (2) massive empty whitespace above header from admin section, (3) logo/nav/actions misaligned, (4) full blog title renders instead of short brand name
error_database.root_cause: (1) LinkList widgets lacked b:if cond='data:link.name' guard, (2) admin Navbar section placed before header — even with CSS hiding, can cause flash/whitespace, (3) header used LEFT+nav+RIGHT layout pushing nav next to logo, (4) <data:blog.title/> outputs full blog title
error_database.bad_code_pattern: 1) without name guard 2) admin section above header 3) nav inside header-left with logo 4)
error_database.fix: 1) Added guard on all LinkList renders 2) Moved to AFTER 3) Restructured to LEFT(logo) / CENTER(nav) / RIGHT(actions) with .header-center 4) Replaced with hardcoded 'RankrSEO' for brand display 5) Removed container class from header-inner to avoid double padding
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-022: LinkList link rendering must always guard with b:if cond='data:link.name'; admin section must never precede header; brand text must be short hardcoded value, not dynamic title

error_database.issue: Internal theme configuration variables rendered in UI (header/footer)
error_database.symptom: LinkList widgets display config variable names as visible menu items: disqusShortname, commentsSystem, noThumb, fixedSidebar, fixedMenu, showMoreText, postPerPage, 'Mega Menu'
error_database.root_cause: LinkList widgets bind to data:links which resolves from Blogger Dashboard link entries. Config variable names entered as link names in the Dashboard render unconditionally through the widget loop.
error_database.fix:
1) Header nav: replaced LinkList widget with hardcoded <a> links (Option A)
2) Mobile nav: replaced LinkList widget with hardcoded <a> links (Option A)
3) Footer columns (x5): replaced all LinkList widgets with locked HTML widgets containing hardcoded link lists
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-023: Never iterate over theme settings or generic widget settings for UI navigation. Only render explicitly whitelisted menu sources. For header/footer navigation, use hardcoded HTML widgets instead of LinkList widgets to prevent Dashboard config leakage.

error_database.issue: Poor footer component architecture and broken spacing system
error_database.symptom: Massive empty vertical space (120px padding-top + 80px margin-bottom), social icons positioned as floating separate section outside brand column, grid unbalanced, missing column headings, cluttered bottom bar with two separate widget sections, weak typography hierarchy
error_database.root_cause: Footer was built without a coherent layout architecture: excessive fixed spacing values, social icons in separate b:section outside brand column flow, copyright and legal links in separate widgets, typography used generic token values without hierarchy
error_database.bad_code_pattern:
padding: var(--space-6xl) 0 0 (120px)
.footer-grid { margin-bottom: var(--space-5xl) } (80px extra)
.footer-social as independent div outside .footer-brand
footer h4 { text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing } instead of clean heading
Two separate b:sections for bottom bar (copyright + legal links)
error_database.fix:
1) Reduced footer padding from 120px→80px, removed grid margin-bottom
2) Merged social icons into brand widget content (eliminated separate footer-social section)
3) Merged copyright + legal links into single bottom bar widget
4) Brand logo 1.5rem→1.75rem (28px), column headings 16px→18px semibold (no uppercase), links 14px
5) Added footer-bottom margin-top: 80px for visual separation
6) Removed arrow pseudo-elements on link hover for cleaner look
7) All sections changed to locked='true' + showaddelement='no'
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-024: Footer must use 2fr/1fr/1fr/1fr grid with 64px gap. Social icons embedded in brand column. Copyright + legal links in single bottom bar widget. No fixed heights — content-driven spacing. Typography: brand 28px, headings 18px semibold, links 14px.

error_database.issue: Footer, blog, and contact sections look inconsistent and low quality
error_database.symptom: Sections designed in isolation with no shared design tokens for spacing, typography, or component patterns. Footer spacing excessive, blog cards weak, contact section flat. No visual consistency across the theme.
error_database.root_cause: No global spacing, typography, and component token system was defined before building individual sections. Each section was styled independently using ad-hoc values.
error_database.bad_code_pattern: Individual sections use different padding values (120px vs 80px vs 64px), inconsistent border radii (radius-lg vs radius-xl vs radius-2xl), mismatched title/body font sizes, and no shared component pattern for cards, inputs, or buttons across sections.
error_database.fix:
1) Defined 40+ design tokens in :root (--space-*, --text-*, --shadow-*, --radius-*, --leading-*, --tracking-*, --transition-*)
2) Applied tokens uniformly across footer (padding, gap, margins), blog (card radius, shadows, spacing), contact (grid gap, card padding, input height)
3) Established consistent component patterns: cards use surface+border+shadow, inputs use 60px height+radius-lg+2px border, buttons use gradient+radius-lg+weight 700
4) Harmonized typography: headings 18px semibold across sections, body 14-16px, links 14px
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-025: Never design individual sections before defining global design tokens. All spacing, typography, border radii, shadows, and transitions must reference CSS custom properties from :root. Section-specific overrides are only permitted for layout structure (grid, positioning) — never for raw aesthetic values.

error_database.issue: Two <b:includable id='postAuthor'> in Blog1 widget — empty one at line 2989 shadows real one at line 1644 (defaultmarkup)
error_database.symptom: Post author name never renders on any page; empty includable takes precedence
error_database.root_cause: An empty <b:includable id='postAuthor'/> was accidentally placed in Blog1 widget during includable list compilation, overriding the fully-implemented version from defaultmarkup
error_database.bad_code_pattern: <b:includable id='postAuthor'/> (empty self-closing tag) — shadows the real <b:includable id='postAuthor' var='post'> in defaultmarkup
error_database.fix: Replaced empty includable with full implementation: author name display inside <span itemprop='name'> with !data:view.isPage guard
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-026: Every includable override in a widget must have a complete implementation, never empty unless explicitly intended to disable the feature

error_database.issue: Duplicate BlogPosting JSON-LD schema in Blog1 widget — defined in both 'post' and 'postMeta' includables
error_database.symptom: Google Search Console warns 'Multiple schema.org markup blocks with same type' on single post pages
error_database.root_cause: Blog1 widget defined BlogPosting schema in <b:includable id='post'> (line 2853) AND in <b:includable id='postMeta'> (line 3102). While postMeta is currently unreachable in the Blog1 rendering chain, the duplicate definition creates confusion and risk if postMeta is later referenced.
error_database.bad_code_pattern:
<b:includable id='postMeta' var='post'>
  <script type='application/ld+json'>{...BlogPosting schema...}</script>
</b:includable>
+ same schema in <b:includable id='post'>
error_database.fix: Replaced postMeta's full schema with <b:includable id='postMeta'><b:comment>Disabled — schema in post includable</b:comment></b:includable>
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-027: JSON-LD schema for a given type (BlogPosting, BreadcrumbList, etc.) must be defined in exactly one includable per page. Use comments to document which includable has the canonical schema.

error_database.issue: No responsive breakpoint for very small phones (≤360px width)
error_database.symptom: On iPhone 5/SE and other small devices (320-360px), content overflows horizontally, hero text gets clipped, service cards too wide for viewport
error_database.root_cause: Smallest breakpoint was 480px; no CSS overrides for devices narrower than 480px
error_database.fix: Added @media (max-width: 360px) breakpoint with reduced hero padding (56px 0 24px), smaller container padding (0 12px), smaller headings (clamp(1.2rem, 7vw, 1.4rem)), reduced card padding, smaller font sizes across all components
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-028: The responsive breakpoint spectrum must include a ≤360px tier for very small phones. At minimum, override: container padding, hero padding/headings, card padding, section padding.

error_database.issue: Hero vertical padding (120px) excessive on mobile screens
error_database.symptom: Hero section uses 56% of viewport height on a 375px phone; 240px of combined padding leaves only 135px for content
error_database.root_cause: Hero padding was set uniformly for desktop: 120px top / 64px bottom with only a modest reduction at 768px (80px / 40px)
error_database.fix: Added media query overrides: @media (max-width: 480px) { .hero { padding: 64px 0 32px; } } @media (max-width: 360px) { .hero { padding: 56px 0 24px; } }
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-029: Hero section must have responsive padding at every breakpoint: desktop 120px/64px, 768px 80px/40px, 480px 64px/32px, 360px 56px/24px.

error_database.issue: Large CSS filter blur values (80-100px) cause janky scrolling on mobile
error_database.symptom: Hero gradient orbs with blur(80px) and CTA orbs with blur(100px) cause visible frame drops on mobile browsers; compositing cost high for large blurred elements
error_database.root_cause: Decoratives orbs sized 400-600px with heavy blur filters force mobile GPU into expensive offscreen rendering pass
error_database.bad_code_pattern: .hero-svg-bg .gradient-orb { filter: blur(80px); } and .cta-svg-bg .gradient-orb { filter: blur(100px); } with large element dimensions (600px, 400px)
error_database.fix: Reduced filter blur from 80px→60px (hero) and 100px→60px (CTA); reduced orb dimensions: orb-1 from 600px→400px, orb-2 from 400px→300px; reduced opacity from 0.15→0.1 (hero) and 0.1→0.08 (CTA)
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-030: CSS filter blur on decorative elements must not exceed 60px. Orb/element dimensions should be ≤400px. Combined with reduced opacity to maintain visual quality while keeping compositing cost manageable on mobile GPUs.

error_database.issue: Duplicate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks scattered across CSS, leading to maintenance confusion
error_database.symptom: Three separate 480px breakpoint blocks exist: one main block (line 920), one for author box only (line 1051), and one for related posts (line 1075). Same rules could conflict or be missed.
error_database.root_cause: Component-specific responsive rules added as separate blocks during iterative development instead of being consolidated into the main breakpoint blocks
error_database.fix: Merged author box rules (.oixvV-author, .author-social) and related posts rules (.Super-related) into the main 480px breakpoint block. Removed duplicate standalone media queries. Removed duplicate 480px query for .Super-related that mirrored 768px rule.
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-031: All responsive rules for a given breakpoint must live in exactly one @media block. Component-specific overrides should be added to the shared block (with a comment grouping if helpful), never as separate standalone media queries.

error_database.issue: Service card gradient border uses mask-composite: exclude, unsupported in Firefox
error_database.symptom: Firefox renders no border on service cards because mask-composite: exclude is not supported, so the entire pseudo-element is masked away
error_database.root_cause: mask-composite is a relatively new CSS feature; Firefox supports mask-composite via -webkit-mask-composite but not the standard property, and 'exclude' behavior differs across implementations
error_database.fix: Added @supports not (mask-composite: exclude) block that removes the mask and instead uses a simple border-bottom with primary-color at 0.3 opacity on hover, providing a visual indicator without the gradient effect
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-032: Any CSS feature that uses mask-composite, conic-gradient, or other relatively new CSS features must have a @supports not (...) fallback that provides a graceful degraded experience.

error_database.issue: Featured post cards missing fallback background when image is absent
error_database.symptom: If a featured post has no image, the featured-card element has no visible background, showing an empty/blank space instead of a placeholder
error_database.root_cause: featured-card relied entirely on the post thumbnail image for visual content; no background property was set as fallback
error_database.bad_code_pattern: .featured-card { position: relative; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); overflow: hidden; min-height: 400px; } /* no background */
error_database.fix: Added background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--bg-alt), var(--border-light)) fallback to featured-card so that even without an image the card is visually present
error_database.prevention_rule: RULE-033: Any container that relies on an image for visual content must have a background fallback (gradient, solid color, or placeholder) so it remains visible when the image is missing or fails to load.
